subject: Statistics
title: Residual Analysis
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Residual Analysis.

hook.text: After removing trend and seasonality, what's left should be random noise. Patterns mean a missed signal.

buildup.text: Residuals are the leftover after subtracting the modeled components. They should be random and uncorrelated.

discovery.text: Patterns in residuals signal a missed component — perhaps a cycle, a structural break, or a nonlinear relationship.

twist.text: The Ljung-Box test checks if residual autocorrelations are zero. Failure means the model is incomplete.

climax.text: Good residual analysis is the quality control of forecasting. Every model should pass this check.

punchline.text: If the residuals have patterns, the model has gaps. Noise should be noisy.

quiz.question: What does it mean if residuals from a time series model show patterns?
options: The model missed a systematic component — it's incomplete and should be improved
The model is perfect and the patterns are coincidence
Residuals always have patterns regardless of model quality
